title: OSCORE option parsing divergence: reserved-bit laxity (C+Rust), k=0 trailing bytes (C rejects/Rust accepts), id_context unusable in C and Rust
description: (1) Reserved bits: RFC 8613 6.1 requires bits 5-7 (0xE0) zero; C checks only 0x80 (oscore.c:1028); Rust only 0x80 (lib.rs:719, inside 2auf.48.3 scope); aiocoap checks 0xC0 + treats 0x20 as group flag. C-side laxity untracked. (2) Trailing bytes with k=0: C REJECTS (oscore.c:1074-1076); Rust silently accepts and discards (lib.rs:750-758). A C node rejects some messages a Rust node accepts. (3) id_context: Python fully supports (C.3/C.6 vectors pass); C has struct fields but NO API to set them - oscore_ctx_create derives keys with ctx->id_context right after memset (oscore.c:589-621), always empty; Rust Context::new takes no id_context at all (vectors test skips those). C.3/C.6-style contexts impossible in C and Rust. Fix all three; wire the id_context vectors once support exists.
